title: Dried rose and orris-root sachet
subtitle: A fragrance sachet with dried rose petals, orris root, and rose geranium oil for drawers and wardrobes
excerpt: Orris root fixes the volatile oils in the rose petals, extending the scent for months. A few drops of rose geranium freshen the blend. Tuck into drawers, wardrobes, or linen cupboards. The sachet can be refreshed with a few more drops of essential oil as the scent fades.
sourceNotes: Orris root as a fragrance fixative: standard perfumery reference. Dried rose petals in potpourri: traditional botanical fragrance craft.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale up to fill as many sachets as needed. The orris-to-petals ratio stays the same.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make batches in advance; the fragrance improves slightly over the first two weeks as the scent components meld.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: The dried petals last indefinitely in a cloth sachet. The scent fades after 6 to 12 months -- refresh by opening the sachet and adding a few drops of essential oil, then resealing.

glossaryTerms.definition: Powdered dried rhizome of Iris germanica. Used as a fragrance fixative in sachets and potpourri -- it binds to volatile aromatic compounds and releases them slowly, extending the life of the scent blend.

content.text: The dried rose petals carry the scent and the visual appeal;

acts as a fixative, binding to the volatile aromatic oils in the petals and releasing them slowly. This combination keeps the sachet fragrant for months rather than weeks. The rose geranium essential oil adds intensity and longevity to the natural rose note.

items.prepNote: cosmetic or food-grade; not florist roses
20 cm lengths, to tie the sachets

content.text: Combine the dried rose petals and orris root powder in a bowl and toss gently to distribute the powder through the petals. Add the rose geranium essential oil, drop by drop, tossing between additions. Leave the mixture in the bowl for 10 minutes to allow the orris root to absorb the oil.
Divide the mixture between two muslin squares. Gather the edges and tie firmly with ribbon, trapping the petals inside. The sachet should hold its shape without any petals escaping through the weave.

Place in a drawer, between folded linens, or in the corner of a wardrobe. Gently squeeze the sachet when you want a burst of fragrance. Keep away from direct contact with light-coloured fabrics -- the rose petals can transfer colour if the sachet gets damp.

When the scent fades after several months, untie the sachet, add 5 to 10 drops of rose geranium or lavender essential oil to the dried blend, toss to distribute, and retie. The orris root will absorb and re-fix the new oil. Most sachets can be refreshed two or three times before the petals become too faded and dry to carry any scent.
